item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wonderful little article. I enjoy the simplicity of it all. +1

Also, did anyone else read the "alien's" dialogue in the voice of J K Simmons? 'Cause I did, lol.

item.title: satellite disc?
item.content:encoded:
I'm assuming it should read satellite dish?
Satellite disc isn't a thing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just went and read that… Yes, I goddamn hope so xD That thing was great. It does have a resemblance though, i can see the connection.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I mean yea, its LOLzy but I like it, It adds some funniness too it. While still upholding the fact that the things sending them look down upon us. Lol i wouldn't want anymore to be done to this, maybe grammatical things but The jokes are gewd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Any connection to Beauremont?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This just tickles me. Aliens powering their planet with turbines dropped on other planets is great. But the best part is theri reaction to it being disconnected. The confetti and noisemakers is priceless. That image made my day. I'm also quite amused that they have no idea if we can understand them.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just what I had in my head as well!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Niiiiiiiice.
So these things are having us power their planet except they don't understand us lol
Epic failure on their part. Also don't they realize that humans are far too curious to take "Go away (angry demon drawing)" for an answer?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry, I feex.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You still have not fixed the "similar to plastic" line. Plastic is a class of synthetic organic, mold able compounds.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Works for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I got rid of the implications for using it as power, but personally I just want to keep the note there to clarify why they're still powering this thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, but you know that.

The only thing I'd lose is the note at the end. It doesn't add anything and it kind of implies we're even thinking a little bit about powering sites.

Still, you got my +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very enjoyable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Better. So much better. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh. I'm not feeling it. The… whatever they are seem kind of cliche. Tentative downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed, and thanks. :D

E: should also mention that Crayne made that line. Props to him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
standard black paint

There is no standard black paint. I suggest changing that to "unremarkable".

Otherwise this was a lulzworthy look at aliens trying to manipulate us while having only the barest understanding of how humans work. The "Do not attempt to eat, mate, or engage in combat with MK.VI Turbine. Just leave it alone." line in particular was fantastic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
3. Do not attempt to eat, mate, or engage in combat with MK.VI Turbine. Just leave it alone.

This is probably too LOLfoundation to actually go in the article, buuuut…

Addendum: On ██/██/████, Dr. Edison approached SCP-1920 in plate armor wielding a medieval broadsword and attempted to challenge SCP-1920 to armed combat. Dr. Edison was subsequently apprehended by security personnel, and transferred to Site ██ to undergo counseling for a previously-undiagnosed stress disorder. SCP-1920 has yet to respond to Dr. Edison's request.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much improved from last time. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You fixed everything I didn't like last time 'round and polished the rest.

Take your damn upvote. You've earned it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Looks good, upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixing that first part, but in argument of the second point, I'd say it was"unknown" at the time of the writing of this article by scientists, which was most likely after its immediate discovery. But if it bothers you, I'll change it to "undiscovered".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Its blades consist of a single piece of unknown polymer material similar to plastic

Two problems with this; firstly, it should be 'an unknown polymer'.

Secondly: similar to plastic? If one is being scientific enough to use the term polymer, surely one would give a specific type of plastic rather than simply saying 'plastic'. It also seems a little unnecessary to make this material so mysterious, why not just define it?

Furthermore, I'm not sure you can have an unknown polymer. You take a sample, look at it, name it and now you have a new polymer that is now known. You could say an unencountered polymer but it can never remain unknown unless something is stopping the Foundation from taking a sample.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: With the changes, I'm happy enough to upvote. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The people who made this thing sent more when they stopped getting power from it, which isn't exactly a good thing for the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not entirely sure why the Foundation keeps them powered, but amusing nonetheless. Cautious upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ok, thanks for explaining it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Even if it produces electricity at an irregular rate (i.e. the power output is irregular), it'll still produce an amount of energy over a given time period. And in terms of the effect you want (i.e. at periodic milestones the SCP makes an encouraging noise) it makes much more sense for that to be after it produced a set amount of energy, rather than just because it was briefly at a particular threshold rate of energy production, which only occurs randomly anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed the things you pointed out (except the dialogue. I'm not sure how or what it reveals first, maybe their condescending attitude of us?)

Edit: wait, this produces electricity irregularly, wouldn't rates not apply to this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was Meserach, yes. :)

I'm pleased you went with the scary-face idea, but I think this still needs a little more work before I'd upvote it.

+ show block
– hide block

The rotational speed of its blades does not correlate with it's level of output

"its"

Once 1,000 watts of power have been produced

Physics error: power is a measure of energy produced over time, so it's a rate rather than a quantity. As such, you can't say that "1,000 watts of power have been produced": you need to give a measure of energy instead, such as "1 kilowatt-hour of energy has been produced" (which is the energy produced from a generator producing 1,000 watts for an hour).

As of ██/██/19██, SCP-1920 is to be kept actively generating electrivity; see Revised Containment Procedures.

"electricity", obv.

I also feel like the plaque and the dialogue give the game away a little too much, ahead of the "scary face" payoff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cute! Look at the cute little eldritch Joe-blows!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a rewrite of the turbine SCP I had submitted a while back, it should look familiar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why does this look familiar?

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Special thanks goes to Meserach (I think?), Crayne, DrKens, and anyone else who looked at this in chat for helping me with this.

Image was taken by myself.

Feel free to read Radiance and Cachinnation for more on LCE.
